name: Serra Gelada Traverse Loop
name_original: Trail run por el Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada
description: Tackle a difficult 11.0-mile (17.7 km) trail run in Serra Gelada Natural Park, gaining 2391 feet (729 metres) with stunning coastal views.
region_intro: Jogging around Benidorm offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by extensive coastal promenades along its beaches and the more challenging terrains of the Sierra Gelada Natural Park. The region features significant elevation changes, particularly in the natural park, providing panoramic coastal views from its cliffs and hills. Urban green spaces like L'Aigüera Park also offer tranquil settings for lighter jogs. This combination of seaside paths, mountainous trails, and city parks caters to various fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Benidorm The most popular jogging route is Serra Gelada Traverse Loop, a 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain within the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Avenida del Rey Jaime I – Plaza de la Cruz loop from Benidorm, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.7 km) path. This route offers a mix of urban and coastal scenery with moderate climbs. Local runners also love the View of Benidorm's skyline – Benidorm Marina loop from Castellum Romano, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas and offering city views, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Benidorm is defined by coastal promenades, challenging mountain trails, and urban green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy seaside runs to difficult climbs. The routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 7900 runners have used komoot to explore Benidorm's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Benidorm?
A: Benidorm offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 90 routes available on komoot. These range from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain trails, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Benidorm?
A: Benidorm boasts an exceptional climate with mild winters and minimal rainfall, making it ideal for year-round outdoor exercise. Early mornings or late afternoons are generally best to avoid the midday sun, especially during the warmer months.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Benidorm has plenty of options for an easy jog. The extensive promenades along Playa de Levante and Playa de Poniente offer over 9.5 km of flat, pedestrian-friendly routes with stunning sea views. For a tranquil setting, L'Aigüera Park in the city center provides a lighter jog amidst gardens and fountains. There are 12 easy routes listed on komoot.

Q: Where can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation gain?
A: For a more challenging experience, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Gelada Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice. It features diverse terrains with substantial elevation gain and panoramic coastal views. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872514591/serra-gelada-traverse-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Gelada Traverse Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 17.7 km with over 700 meters of elevation gain. Komoot lists 24 difficult routes in the area.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Benidorm?
A: Many of Benidorm's running routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33815012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benidorm Marina – View of Benidorm's skyline loop from Benidorm Intermodal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7 km trail that offers city and coastal views.

Q: What kind of viewpoints can I expect on Benidorm's running trails?
A: Benidorm's routes offer breathtaking viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Gelada Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; provides stunning coastal vistas from its cliffs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Benidorm's skyline – Benidorm Marina loop from Castellum Romano&lt;/a&gt; offers fantastic city views. You can also find iconic spots like the Balcón del Mediterráneo and Tossal de la Cala, which provide panoramic views of the city, beaches, and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any running routes that are easily accessible by public transport?
A: Many routes in Benidorm are accessible via public transport, especially those starting within the city center or near major transport hubs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33815012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benidorm Marina – View of Benidorm's skyline loop from Benidorm Intermodal&lt;/a&gt; starts conveniently from Benidorm Intermodal.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by interesting natural attractions like waterfalls?
A: While not directly on the main jogging routes within Benidorm, the nearby &lt;strong&gt;Algar Waterfalls&lt;/strong&gt; are a popular natural attraction about 15km away. You can explore the rocky path along the River Algar, passing waterfalls and lagoons. For more information, check out the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls highlight&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Benidorm?
A: The running routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Over 7,900 runners have used komoot to explore Benidorm's varied terrain, often praising the diverse options from scenic coastal paths to challenging mountain trails and the stunning views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Benidorm?
A: Yes, for families looking for a pleasant jog, the flat and wide promenades along Playa de Levante and Playa de Poniente are ideal. These routes are stroller-friendly and offer easy access to amenities. L'Aigüera Park also provides a safe and enjoyable environment for a family run.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for endurance training?
A: Absolutely. For long-distance runners, the combined length of the coastal promenades offers extensive mileage. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33811723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón del Mediterráneo – Plaza de la Cruz loop from Mundomar&lt;/a&gt;, at 17.5 km, provide a substantial distance with varied terrain and beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles visible from the running routes?
A: While many routes offer views of Benidorm's modern skyline and the historic Balcón del Mediterráneo, for more distinct historical landmarks, you might venture slightly further. The region boasts several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a glimpse into the area's rich history, though it requires a short trip from Benidorm.
tips: Beautiful view over the bay of Benidorm. Steep approach, fitness required, but doable. At the viewpoint you are rewarded with a scenic experience that you cannot resist. The sea glitters, there is a breathtaking view of the coast of Benidorm and the Sierra Gelada. Inland you can see the rugged landscape of the Puig Campana and the Ailtana mountain range.
The altitude of Alto del Gobernador is about 435 meters. We will reach the top after 2,100 meters of route from the start of the Sierra Helada Natural Park.

The Albir lighthouse is located at the end of the paved road in the Serra Gelada. From here you can see the Bombarda point, the Bombarda tower and the beautiful cliff.

Currently the lighthouse is used as an interpretation center.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail run?
faqs.answer: This trail run is rated as difficult. With a distance of approximately 17.7 km and an elevation gain of around 729 meters, it features significant ascents and descents, rocky sections, and varied terrain. It is best suited for experienced trail runners with good fitness and appropriate footwear.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, ranging from natural paths and unpaved surfaces to significant rocky sections with loose stones. You'll encounter steep ascents and descents, and some parts may require sure-footedness. The route showcases fossil dunes, ancient limestone, and Mediterranean flora.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this trail run?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass several notable landmarks. These include The Cross of Benidorm, offering panoramic views, Governor's Height, and the historic Albir Lighthouse. The trail also provides breathtaking coastal views and dramatic cliffs.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this trail run?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, but the Serra Gelada Natural Park has various access points. For routes starting near Benidorm, parking can often be found near the Benidorm Cross area. For routes closer to Albir, parking is available near the Albir Lighthouse trailhead. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, as parking can fill up quickly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees for Serra Gelada Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or permits required to access the Serra Gelada Natural Park for trail running or hiking. It is a publicly accessible natural park.

faqs.question: Is this trail run dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Serra Gelada Natural Park is generally dog-friendly. However, dogs must be kept on a leash at all times to protect the park's wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Be mindful of the challenging terrain and potential heat, especially for your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to do this trail run?
faqs.answer: The best time of year for this trail run is during the cooler months, from autumn to spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making the difficult terrain even more challenging. Starting early in the morning is recommended to avoid midday heat, regardless of the season.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several official trails within the park. You will find yourself on sections of El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, [CIMA AT06] Serra Gelata, Camí de l'Àngel, Assagador Reial, Costera de la Salpa, Camí de la Cantera, Camí dels Torrers, and Cami dels Torrers.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this trail run?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, as there are no drinking fountains in the park. Appropriate trail running shoes with good grip are essential for the rocky and uneven terrain. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) and a fully charged phone are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no drinking fountains or water sources directly on the trail within the Serra Gelada Natural Park. It is crucial to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your run, especially considering the distance and elevation gain.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trailheads?
faqs.answer: While there are no facilities directly on the trail, you'll find plenty of cafes, restaurants, and pubs in the nearby towns of Benidorm, L'Alfàs del Pi, and Altea. These towns are easily accessible from various park entrances and offer options for refreshments before or after your run.
